title: 10 best herbal capsules for acid reflux
intro_desc: Herbal capsules for acid reflux are dietary supplements that contain a combination of natural herbs, which can help alleviate the symptoms of acid reflux. These herbs include chamomile, licorice root, marshmallow root, and slippery elm. They work by reducing inflammation, coating the esophageal lining, and improving digestion, thereby preventing the backflow of stomach acid. For instance, licorice root has been shown to increase the mucus lining of the esophagus, providing a protective barrier against acid. By taking herbal capsules, individuals can experience relief from acid reflux symptoms, improved digestion, and an overall better quality of life. With fewer disruptions from acid reflux, individuals can enjoy their meals, have better sleep, and engage in daily activities without discomfort.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tarragon capsule helps with acid reflux because it contains active compounds that can aid in digestion and soothe the gastrointestinal tract. Tarragon has been shown to stimulate the production of digestive enzymes, which can help break down food and reduce the production of stomach acid. Additionally, tarragon has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the esophagus and stomach lining, providing relief from the symptoms of acid reflux. Furthermore, tarragon is known to have a calming effect on the digestive system, which can help prevent the occurrence of acid reflux.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Tarragon has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the esophagus caused by acid reflux, providing relief from symptoms. This property is due to the presence of flavonoids and volatile oils.

Antispasmodic: Tarragon has antispasmodic properties that can help relax the gastrointestinal muscles, reducing the likelihood of acid reflux and easing discomfort. The active constituents responsible for this property are methyl chavicol and estragole.

Carminative: Tarragon has carminative properties that can help reduce gas and bloating, which can exacerbate acid reflux symptoms. This property is due to the presence of volatile oils such as estragole and cineole.

Digestive stimulant: Tarragon has digestive stimulant properties that can help improve digestion and absorption of nutrients, reducing the likelihood of acid reflux. The active constituents responsible for this property are the bitter compounds found in tarragon.

Diuretic: Tarragon has diuretic properties that can help eliminate excess fluids and toxins from the body, reducing the pressure on the digestive system and alleviating acid reflux. This property is due to the presence of flavonoids and volatile oils.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of the tarragon plant are commonly used in medicinal tea for acid reflux due to their anti-inflammatory properties which can help reduce irritation in the esophagus.
Stems: The stems of the tarragon plant contain calming compounds that can help soothe the digestive system, making it an effective ingredient in medicinal tea for acid reflux.
Flowers: The flowers of the tarragon plant have antispasmodic properties, which can help relax the muscles in the digestive tract and alleviate symptoms of acid reflux.
Seeds: The seeds of the tarragon plant contain volatile oils that can aid in digestion and reduce symptoms of acid reflux, making them a popular addition to medicinal tea.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2 tablespoons of dried tarragon leaves and 0.5 cup of hot water; steep leaves in hot water for 15-20 minutes to create a tarragon tea infusion.
Strain the tea infusion to separate the liquid from the leaves, then measure 1 cup of the tarragon tea; combine with 0.5 cup of empty vegetarian capsules and a capsule filling machine.
Fill each capsule with the tarragon tea mixture, using the filling machine, ensuring each capsule contains an equal amount of the liquid.
Allow the filled capsules to dry at room temperature for 24-48 hours, until they become completely dry and hard.
Store the dried tarragon capsules in an airtight container in a cool, dry place, taking 1-2 capsules up to three times a day as needed for acid reflux relief.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Common barberry capsule helps with acid reflux because it contains berberine, an alkaloid that can help reduce inflammation and irritation in the digestive tract. This herbal supplement works by strengthening the lower esophageal sphincter, a muscle that acts as a barrier between the stomach and the esophagus. By enhancing the function of this muscle, common barberry capsule can prevent the backflow of stomach acid into the esophagus, thereby alleviating the symptoms of acid reflux. Additionally, barberry has antibacterial properties that can help eliminate harmful bacteria in the gut, further supporting digestive health and function.
remedy_properties: Antagonist of H2 receptors: Barberry contains berberine, which acts as an antagonist of H2 receptors, reducing acid production and helping to prevent acid reflux.
Gastroprotective effects: Barberry has gastroprotective properties, which can help to protect the lining of the stomach and esophagus from acid damage.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Barberry has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce inflammation in the esophagus and stomach, providing relief from acid reflux symptoms.
Promotes gut motility: Barberry stimulates the muscles of the gastrointestinal tract, promoting gut motility and helping to prevent acid reflux.
Antimicrobial properties: Barberry has antimicrobial properties, which can help to eliminate harmful bacteria in the gut, reducing the risk of acid reflux caused by bacterial overgrowth.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Contain high levels of tannins, which can help reduce inflammation and irritation in the esophagus caused by acid reflux.
Bark: Rich in alkaloids, which can help neutralize stomach acid and provide relief from acid reflux symptoms.
Rhyzomes: Contain berberine, an active compound with anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe the digestive tract and alleviate acid reflux.
Seeds: High in fiber, which can help regulate digestion and prevent acid reflux by keeping food moving through the digestive system.
Flowers: Contain flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce irritation and discomfort caused by acid reflux.
remedy_recipe: Gather 1,000 mg of dried common barberry bark and grind it into a fine powder.
Mix the powder with 250 mg of a binding agent, such as microcrystalline cellulose, to ensure the capsule's contents will hold together.
Load the herbal mixture into size "0" vegetarian capsules using a capsule filling machine.
Store the completed capsules in a cool, dry place, away from direct sunlight.
Take one capsule up to three times daily, 30 minutes before meals, or as directed by a healthcare professional.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: White tea capsule helps with acid reflux because it contains high levels of antioxidants, particularly catechins, which can help strengthen the lower esophageal sphincter (LES), a muscle that acts as a barrier between the stomach and the esophagus. A weakened LES can allow stomach acid to flow back up into the esophagus, causing acid reflux. White tea may also help reduce inflammation and soothe the digestive tract, providing relief from symptoms of acid reflux. Additionally, white tea has been shown to have antibacterial properties, which may help eliminate harmful bacteria in the stomach that can contribute to acid reflux.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: White tea has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the esophagus, providing relief from acid reflux symptoms. This is due to the presence of flavonoids such as catechins and epicatechins.

Antioxidant: White tea contains high levels of antioxidants that can help protect the lining of the esophagus from damage caused by acid reflux. The main antioxidants responsible for this property are polyphenols, including epigallocatechin gallate (EGCG).

Astringent: White tea has astringent properties that can help tighten and strengthen the lower esophageal sphincter (LES), preventing acid reflux. The tannins in white tea contribute to its astringent properties.

Antispasmodic: White tea has antispasmodic properties that can help relax the muscles in the gastrointestinal tract, reducing the frequency and severity of acid reflux. This is due to the presence of volatile oils, such as limonene and linalool.

Gastroprotective: White tea has gastroprotective properties that can help protect the stomach lining from damage caused by stomach acid. This is attributed to the presence of methylxanthines, such as theobromine and theophylline.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Contain high levels of antioxidants that can help reduce inflammation and soothe the digestive tract, providing relief for acid reflux symptoms.
Buds: Rich in flavonoids and other compounds that can help strengthen the lower esophageal sphincter, preventing acid reflux.
Stems: May contain anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate symptoms of acid reflux and promote overall digestive health.
Flowers: Can help relax the gastrointestinal muscles, reducing the likelihood of acid reflux and providing a soothing effect on the digestive system.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2 teaspoons of dried organic peppermint leaves, 1 teaspoon of dried organic marshmallow root, and 1 teaspoon of dried organic slippery elm bark.
Grind the herbs into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix 1 teaspoon of the ground herb mixture with 100mg of a plant-based capsule and fill the other half with a filler such as rice flour.
Press the two halves of the capsule together to enclose the herbal mixture.
Take one capsule three times a day, 30 minutes before meals, with a full glass of water.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Caraway capsule helps with acid reflux because it contains caraway oil, which has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat digestive issues. The oil's key compound, carvone, has been found to have antispasmodic properties that can help relax the gastrointestinal tract, reducing the symptoms of acid reflux. Caraway oil may also stimulate the production of digestive enzymes, promoting healthy digestion and reducing the likelihood of acid reflux. Additionally, caraway oil has been shown to have antibacterial and antifungal properties, which can help maintain a healthy gut microbiome and further support digestive health.
remedy_properties: Antacid property: Caraway capsules have the ability to neutralize stomach acid, providing quick relief from acid reflux. This is due to the presence of volatile oils such as carvone and limonene.

Gastroprotective property: Caraway helps protect the stomach lining from damage caused by excessive acid production, reducing the risk of acid reflux. This is attributed to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids.

Spasmolytic property: Caraway can relax the gastrointestinal muscles, preventing acid reflux by reducing the likelihood of acid backing up into the esophagus. This is due to the presence of compounds like carvol and furanocoumarins.

Carminative property: Caraway helps reduce gastric distension and bloating by promoting the expulsion of gas from the gastrointestinal tract, which can help alleviate symptoms of acid reflux. This is attributed to the presence of essential oils like carvone and limonene.
Cholagogue property: Caraway stimulates the production of bile, which aids in digestion and can help prevent acid reflux. This is due to the presence of volatile oils and flavonoids.
remedy_parts: Seeds: Caraway seeds are commonly used in medicinal tea for acid reflux due to their carminative properties, which help to reduce gas and bloating.
Leaves: The leaves of the caraway plant are used in tea to soothe the digestive tract and provide relief from acid reflux symptoms.
Fruits: Caraway fruits are rich in essential oils and are used in medicinal tea to aid digestion and reduce acid reflux.
Rhizomes: The rhizomes of the caraway plant are used in tea to provide anti-inflammatory and antioxidant benefits, which can help to reduce acid reflux symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Measure out 1 tablespoon of dried caraway seeds and grind them into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix the ground caraway seeds with 1 cup of empty vegetarian capsule bodies and fill each capsule with a consistent amount of the herbal powder.
Lay out the filled capsules on a clean surface and allow them to dry for 24 hours to ensure the herbal powder is completely dry.
Store the dried caraway seed capsules in an airtight container in a cool, dry place, away from direct sunlight, for up to 6 months.
Take 1-2 capsules, as needed, 30 minutes before meals or as directed by a healthcare professional, to alleviate acid reflux symptoms.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Gotu kola capsule helps with acid reflux because it contains active compounds that strengthen the lining of the digestive tract, reducing the likelihood of acid flowing back up into the esophagus. This herb has been shown to improve gastric ulcer healing and prevent the formation of new ulcers, making it a natural and effective remedy for acid reflux symptoms. Additionally, gotu kola has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, providing relief from discomfort and pain associated with acid reflux. Its ability to enhance the function of the lower esophageal sphincter also contributes to its effectiveness in managing acid reflux.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Gotu kola has been shown to have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation in the esophagus caused by acid reflux, providing relief from symptoms. This property is due to the presence of triterpenoid saponins, such as asiaticoside, madecassoside, and madasiatic acid.

Mucoprotective: Gotu kola has a mucoprotective effect, which means it can help protect the mucous membrane of the digestive tract from damage caused by acid reflux. This is attributed to the herb's polysaccharide content, particularly the arabinogalactan proteins.

Antioxidant: Gotu kola has potent antioxidant properties, which can help protect cells from damage caused by free radicals, reducing the oxidative stress that can contribute to acid reflux symptoms. This is due to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids in gotu kola.

Vulnerary: Gotu kola has vulnerary properties, which means it can help promote wound healing and tissue repair. This can be beneficial for acid reflux as it may help heal damaged esophageal tissue caused by stomach acid. The triterpenoid saponins and flavonoids in gotu kola contribute to this property.

Anxiolytic: Gotu kola has anxiolytic effects, which can help reduce anxiety and promote relaxation. This can be helpful for acid reflux as stress and anxiety can exacerbate symptoms. The active constituents responsible for this property include triterpene acids, such as asiatic acid and madecassic acid, as well as flavonoids like kaempferol and quercetin.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of gotu kola are commonly used to make medicinal tea for acid reflux due to their anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation in the esophagus.
Stems: The stems of gotu kola contain compounds that can help strengthen the muscles in the digestive tract, preventing acid from backing up into the esophagus.
Rhizomes: Gotu kola rhizomes have been shown to have a protective effect on the mucous membrane of the stomach, making it useful for treating acid reflux.
remedy_recipe: Purchase dried gotu kola herb and size “00” vegetarian capsules, aiming for around 100 capsules.
Grind 1 cup of dried gotu kola into a fine powder, using a clean coffee grinder or a blender, then sift the powder to remove larger particles.
Measure 500mg of the gotu kola powder per capsule using a digital scale, filling empty capsules with a capsule filling machine.
Store the filled capsules in airtight containers, preferably in a cool, dark place, for up to six months.
Take 1-2 capsules, three times a day, 30 minutes before meals, as a natural remedy for acid reflux, or as directed by a healthcare professional.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric capsule helps with acid reflux because it contains curcumin, a potent anti-inflammatory compound that reduces inflammation in the digestive tract, alleviating symptoms of acid reflux. Additionally, turmeric stimulates the production of bile, which aids in digestion and the breakdown of fats, preventing the buildup of stomach acid. Furthermore, its antioxidant properties protect the lining of the stomach and esophagus from damage caused by acid reflux. Therefore, incorporating turmeric capsules into your daily routine can provide relief from acid reflux and promote overall digestive health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Turmeric capsules can reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which is important for acid reflux as it can help alleviate symptoms such as heartburn and irritation. This is due to the active constituent curcumin.

Mucosal healing: Turmeric can promote mucosal healing in the gastrointestinal tract, which is important for acid reflux as it can help repair damaged tissue and prevent further irritation. This is also attributed to curcumin.

Antioxidant: Turmeric has antioxidant properties that can protect the digestive tract from damage caused by free radicals, which is important for acid reflux as it can help reduce inflammation and promote healing. Curcumin is once again responsible for this property.

Antispasmodic: Turmeric can help relax the smooth muscles of the gastrointestinal tract, which is important for acid reflux as it can help prevent the backflow of stomach acid into the esophagus. This property is due to the active constituent curcumin and other volatile oils present in turmeric.

Anti-secretory: Turmeric can inhibit the secretion of gastric acid, which is important for acid reflux as it can help reduce the amount of acid in the stomach and alleviate symptoms. This property is attributed to the active constituents curcumin and other alkaloids present in turmeric.
remedy_parts: Rhizomes: Turmeric's underground stem (rhizome) is used for making medicinal tea for acid reflux due to its anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation in the esophagus.
Leaves: Turmeric leaves are used in making medicinal tea for acid reflux because they contain curcumin, which may help increase gastric mucus secretion and protect the stomach lining.
Flowers: Turmeric flowers are used in medicinal tea for acid reflux as they contain curcumin, which has been shown to have gastroprotective effects and may help prevent gastric ulcers.

Seeds: Turmeric seeds are used in medicinal tea for acid reflux due to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the digestive tract.
remedy_recipe: Measure out 1 tablespoon of dried turmeric root and 1/2 teaspoon of black pepper, then grind them together in a spice grinder for 2 minutes to enhance turmeric's bioavailability.
Mix the ground turmeric and black pepper with 2 tablespoons of organic coconut oil and stir until a thick paste forms, allowing for better absorption of turmeric's active compound.
Fill size "00" empty gelatin or vegetarian capsules with 500mg of the turmeric paste each, using a capsule-filling machine or by hand, ensuring consistent dosage.
Allow the filled capsules to dry at room temperature for 24 hours, then store in an airtight container in a cool, dark place for up to 3 months.
Take 1-2 capsules, as needed, 30 minutes before meals, with a glass of water, for acid reflux relief. Consult a healthcare provider if symptoms persist.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice capsule helps with acid reflux because it contains a substance called glycyrrhizin, which can increase the mucus lining of the esophagus and stomach, providing a protective barrier against stomach acid. This can help to reduce inflammation, prevent damage to the esophagus, and alleviate the symptoms of acid reflux. Additionally, licorice may help to increase the production of mucus in the stomach, which can help to neutralize stomach acid and promote healing of the digestive tract. Furthermore, licorice has been shown to have antibacterial properties, which may help to eliminate the H. pylori bacteria that can contribute to acid reflux.
remedy_properties: Demulcent: Licorice acts as a demulcent by forming a protective film on the mucous membranes, which helps to soothe and protect the digestive tract lining from acid reflux. This property is due to the presence of glycyrrhizin and other polysaccharides.

Anti-inflammatory: Licorice has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, providing relief from acid reflux symptoms. The active constituents responsible for this property include flavonoids, coumarins, and triterpenoids.

Mucosal protective: Licorice helps to regenerate and maintain the mucosal lining of the stomach and duodenum, which can be damaged by acid reflux. The mucosal protective effect is attributed to glycyrrhizin and its metabolite, 18-beta-glycyrrhetinic acid.
Antacid: Licorice has antacid properties that help neutralize stomach acid, reducing the severity of acid reflux. The flavonoids and saponins present in licorice contribute to its antacid activity.

Gastric mucosal defensive factor: Licorice stimulates the production of gastric mucosal defensive factors, which help protect the stomach lining from acid damage. This property is due to the presence of glycyrrhizin and its metabolites.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the licorice capsule plant contain glycyrrhizin, which can help protect the stomach lining and reduce inflammation caused by acid reflux.
Stems: The stems of the plant contain flavonoids and coumarins, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate symptoms of acid reflux.
Leaves: The leaves of the licorice capsule plant contain various antioxidants and have been shown to have a soothing effect on the digestive system, making them useful for treating acid reflux.
Flowers: The flowers of the plant contain essential oils and flavonoids that can help reduce inflammation and provide relief from acid reflux symptoms.

Seeds: The seeds of the licorice capsule plant contain a variety of compounds that can help stimulate the production of mucus in the stomach, which can protect the stomach lining and reduce the symptoms of acid reflux.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2000mg of dried licorice root and a capsule-filling machine.
Grind the licorice root into a fine powder, using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Measure 300mg of the licorice root powder and fill empty capsule halves.
Press the halves together to form a complete capsule.

Repeat steps 3-4 until you have filled the desired number of capsules, storing them in a cool, dry place. Preparation time depends on the speed of grinding and filling, typically taking around 30 minutes. Note: Consult with a healthcare professional before starting any new supplement regimen.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Roselle capsule helps with acid reflux because it contains high levels of antioxidants, vitamins, and minerals that can reduce inflammation and soothe the digestive tract. The plant compounds found in roselle, such as anthocyanins and flavonoids, have been shown to help strengthen the lower esophageal sphincter, preventing stomach acid from flowing back up into the esophagus. Additionally, roselle has natural anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate symptoms of acid reflux, such as heartburn, bloating, and stomach discomfort. By promoting healthy digestion and reducing inflammation, roselle capsules can provide effective relief from acid reflux and support overall gut health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Roselle capsules can reduce inflammation in the esophagus, providing relief from acid reflux symptoms, due to the presence of anthocyanins and other polyphenols.
Mucosal protective: The mucilage content in roselle capsules forms a protective layer on the gastric mucosa, preventing acid reflux damage, and promoting healing.

Antioxidant: Roselle capsules help neutralize free radicals, reducing oxidative stress on the gastrointestinal tract and supporting overall gut health, thanks to the high levels of vitamin C and polyphenols.

Spasmolytic: Roselle capsules can help relax the smooth muscles of the gastrointestinal tract, alleviating spasms and discomfort associated with acid reflux, due to the presence of flavonoids and alkaloids.
Anti-secretory: Roselle capsules may inhibit gastric acid secretion, which can help reduce acid reflux, owing to the presence of organic acids and tannins.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Contain high levels of antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce acid reflux symptoms.
Flowers: Rich in phenolic compounds and flavonoids, they can help soothe the digestive tract and provide relief from acid reflux.
Seeds: High in mucilage, which can form a protective coating on the stomach lining, preventing acid reflux.
Bark: Contains tannins that can help reduce inflammation and provide relief from acid reflux symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2 tablespoons of dried roselle calyces and grind them into a fine powder, taking approximately 10 minutes.
Mix the roselle powder with 1 teaspoon of baking soda, which serves as a natural antacid, in a small bowl.
Fill size "00" empty gelatin capsules with the roselle-baking soda mixture using a capsule-filling machine or by hand.
Seal the capsules and store in a cool, dry place, ensuring they are out of direct sunlight.
Take 2-3 capsules daily as needed for acid reflux relief, or as directed by a healthcare professional.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender capsule helps with acid reflux because it contains active compounds that can help reduce inflammation and muscle spasms in the gastrointestinal tract. These spasms can cause the lower esophageal sphincter to relax, allowing stomach acid to flow back up into the esophagus, causing acid reflux. Lavender has also been shown to have calming and soothing effects on the digestive system, which can help prevent acid reflux symptoms. Additionally, lavender has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that can help protect the lining of the stomach and esophagus from damage caused by acid reflux. Overall, English lavender capsules may be a natural and effective way to manage acid reflux symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: English lavender has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the esophagus caused by acid reflux, providing relief from symptoms. The active constituents responsible for this property include linalyl acetate and linalool.

Spasmolytic: English lavender can help relax the smooth muscles in the gastrointestinal tract, reducing spasms and preventing acid reflux. The active constituents responsible for this property include cineole and camphor.

Carminative: English lavender can help reduce gas and bloating in the digestive tract, which can contribute to acid reflux. The active constituents responsible for this property include linalyl acetate and limonene.

Anxiolytic: English lavender has anxiolytic properties that can help reduce stress and anxiety, which can trigger acid reflux. The active constituents responsible for this property include linalool and lavandulol.

Antacid: English lavender has antacid properties that can help neutralize stomach acid, providing relief from acid reflux symptoms. The active constituents responsible for this property include tannins and flavonoids.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in soothing compounds, the flowers of English lavender capsule plant are used in medicinal tea for acid reflux due to their anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce acid production.
Leaves: Containing calming agents, the leaves of English lavender capsule plant are used in medicinal tea for acid reflux to help relax the gastrointestinal tract and alleviate symptoms.
Stems: Packed with antioxidants, the stems of English lavender capsule plant are used in medicinal tea for acid reflux to help protect the stomach lining from damage caused by acid.
Seeds: High in essential oils, the seeds of English lavender capsule plant are used in medicinal tea for acid reflux to aid digestion and soothe the gastrointestinal tract.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2-3 teaspoons of dried English lavender flowers and 0.5-1 teaspoon of baking soda.
Grind the lavender flowers and baking soda into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Measure out 00-sized empty vegetarian capsules and fill each half with the lavender-baking soda powder.
Combine the halves of each capsule and store in a cool, dry place away from direct sunlight.
Take 1-2 capsules up to three times a day, 20-30 minutes before meals, with a full glass of water.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: High mallow capsule helps with acid reflux because it contains mucilage, a substance that becomes slippery when mixed with water. This slippery quality allows the mucilage to provide a protective coating for the esophagus, reducing irritation and inflammation caused by acid reflux. Additionally, high mallow has been shown to have anti-inflammatory properties, which can further soothe the digestive tract and alleviate symptoms of acid reflux. Furthermore, high mallow may help to increase the production of mucus in the stomach, which can help to neutralize stomach acid and prevent acid reflux from occurring.
remedy_properties: Demulcent: High mallow capsule has a soothing effect on the mucous membranes of the gastrointestinal tract, creating a protective barrier against stomach acid, which is important for acid reflux as it can help prevent damage to the esophagus caused by stomach acid. The mucilage content of high mallow gives it this demulcent property.

Anti-inflammatory: High mallow has been shown to reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which is important for acid reflux as it can help alleviate symptoms such as heartburn and pain. The flavonoids and phenolic acids in high mallow give it this anti-inflammatory property.

Antacid: High mallow has the ability to neutralize stomach acid, which is important for acid reflux as it can help reduce the amount of acid in the stomach and prevent reflux. The mucilage and pectin content of high mallow give it this antacid property.

Mucoprotective: High mallow can help protect and heal the mucous membranes of the gastrointestinal tract, which is important for acid reflux as it can help repair damage caused by stomach acid. The mucilage and polysaccharides in high mallow give it this mucoprotective property.

Spasmolytic: High mallow can help relax the smooth muscles of the gastrointestinal tract, which is important for acid reflux as it can help prevent spasms and contractions that can cause acid reflux. The flavonoids and alkaloids in high mallow give it this spasmolytic property.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Contain high levels of tannins and flavonoids that can help reduce inflammation and irritation in the esophagus.
Flowers: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that can help soothe the digestive tract and alleviate acid reflux symptoms.
Seeds: Contain mucilage, which can form a protective coating on the esophageal lining and help reduce acid reflux.
Bark: Has anti-spasmodic properties that can help relax the muscles in the digestive tract and reduce acid reflux.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2 tablespoons of dried high mallow herb and grind it into a fine powder, which may take 5-10 minutes.
Mix the ground high mallow with 1 tablespoon of tapioca starch or another plant-based binder, ensuring thorough combination.
Divide the mixture into 10 equal portions, filling size "00" vegetarian capsules with each portion.
Allow the filled capsules to dry for 24 hours, placed on a clean surface in a cool, dark area.
Store the dried high mallow capsules in an airtight container for up to six months, keeping them in a cool, dark location.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat acid reflux with herbal capsules is by using natural remedies with proven effectiveness. Look for products containing slippery elm, marshmallow root, or licorice root. These herbs create a protective coating in the esophagus, reducing irritation. Additionally, chamomile and catnip can help decrease acid production, while ginger can soothe the digestive tract. Always follow dosage instructions and consult a healthcare professional if symptoms persist or worsen.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to acid reflux that are treated with herbal capsules include functional dyspepsia and gastroesophageal reflux disease (GERD). Herbal remedies such as licorice, chamomile, and marshmallow are often used in capsule form to soothe digestion, reduce inflammation, and alleviate symptoms like heartburn, bloating, and indigestion. Additionally, herbs like slippery elm and turmeric may help repair the damaged lining of the esophagus and stomach, providing relief for individuals with acid reflux-like conditions. However, it is crucial to consult with a healthcare professional before starting any new herbal supplement regimen.
